risks.risk_name: Broad Cross-Departmental Data Access
risks.description: Customer financial data including account details and personal information is accessible across multiple departments (Customer Care and Retention teams) without clear business justification for all access scenarios.

risks.risk_name: Comprehensive Financial Profile Exposure
risks.description: Agents have immediate access to extensive customer financial profiles including loan amounts, payment history, overdue status, and co-applicant details without verification of legitimate business need for each data element during the interaction.

risks.risk_name: Broad Financial Data Exposure to Multiple Teams
risks.description: Comprehensive customer financial data including loan account numbers, sanctioned amounts, outstanding principal, EMI amounts, and payment history is accessible to both Customer Care and Retention teams through Salesforce, potentially beyond what's necessary for their respective functions.

risks.risk_name: Uncontrolled Cross-System Data Integration
risks.description: Customer data flows automatically between Ameyo IVR system and Salesforce without clear visibility into data handling controls, encryption, or access logging across the integration.

risks.risk_name: Cross-Department Data Access Without Business Justification
risks.description: Customer financial data including account details and closure reasons are accessible to both Customer Care and Retention teams through shared Salesforce access, potentially beyond what's necessary for each team's functions.

risks.risk_name: Comprehensive Customer Profile Exposure
risks.description: Extensive customer personal and financial information is displayed to agents immediately upon call connection, including co-applicant details and complete payment history, creating risk of unnecessary data exposure.

risks.risk_name: Unmanaged WhatsApp Data on Personal Devices
risks.description: Customer messages containing account numbers and personal details are stored on unmanaged mobile devices without MDM controls, encryption, or backup procedures. Data is permanently lost when devices are wiped.

risks.risk_name: WhatsApp Data Loss Risk
risks.description: Customer WhatsApp conversations containing account numbers and personal details are stored only on physical phones without backup, creating risk of permanent data loss when phones are wiped.

risks.risk_name: Plain Text Customer Data in Escalation Emails
risks.description: Escalation emails contain customer personal data including names, account numbers, and issue descriptions in plain text. This creates exposure risk if email accounts are compromised, as confirmed by pre-detected analysis.

risks.risk_name: Customer Data in Escalation Emails
risks.description: Escalation emails contain customer personal data including names and account numbers in plain text, creating exposure risk if email accounts are compromised.

risks.risk_name: Unmasked Aadhaar Display to All Staff
risks.description: Full 12-digit Aadhaar numbers of both primary applicants and co-applicants are visible to all 45 Customer Care staff in Salesforce without any masking or field-level security controls. This violates Aadhaar Act provisions that restrict storage and display of full Aadhaar numbers.

risks.risk_name: Absence of Field-Level Security Controls
risks.description: Salesforce has no field-level security configured, meaning all Customer Care profiles have identical read access to sensitive customer data regardless of job role requirements.

risks.risk_name: Unrestricted Data Export Capabilities
risks.description: Team Lead and Manager profiles can export entire customer database including PAN, Aadhaar, and financial data to CSV files with no Data Loss Prevention controls.

risks.risk_name: Call Recording Access Bypass
risks.description: Call recordings are accessible to any Salesforce user with case access through embedded recording links, bypassing Ameyo's native access controls and extending access beyond intended users.

risks.risk_name: Unmanaged Mobile Device Storage
risks.description: Customer data including account numbers and personal details are stored on company phones that lack MDM management and proper security controls.

risks.risk_name: PAN Card Images Transmitted via WhatsApp
risks.description: Customers are sending PAN card images through WhatsApp Business for ID verification. These images are stored on unmanaged company phones with no encryption beyond WhatsApp's default protection.

risks.risk_name: Cross-Border Data Transfer via WhatsApp
risks.description: Customer financial inquiries and PAN card images are processed through WhatsApp Business, which involves Meta's global servers and constitutes cross-border data transfer without proper safeguards.

risks.risk_name: Sensitive Personal Information in Case Descriptions
risks.description: Free-text case descriptions contain highly sensitive personal information including health situations, corruption allegations with named employees, and family relationship changes, accessible to all Customer Care staff.

risks.risk_name: Unmanaged Mobile Devices Handling Customer Data
risks.description: Company-issued phones used for WhatsApp Business are not under Mobile Device Management, creating security risks for customer data stored on these devices.

risks.risk_name: PAN Card Images via WhatsApp
risks.description: Customers are sending PAN card images through WhatsApp Business, which are stored on unmanaged devices with no encryption beyond WhatsApp's E2E encryption.

risks.risk_name: Unmanaged WhatsApp Business Devices
risks.description: WhatsApp Business phones handling customer financial data are not under mobile device management, creating security and data governance risks.

risks.risk_name: Indefinite Email Retention in Shared Mailbox
risks.description: Customer emails containing personal and financial information are retained indefinitely in shared mailbox without retention policy or regular purging procedures.

risks.risk_name: Indefinite Email Retention
risks.description: Customer emails in shared mailbox are retained indefinitely without deletion policy, accumulating years of customer correspondence.

risks.risk_name: Broad Access to Historical Customer Communications
risks.description: Four personnel have unrestricted access to years of customer email history in shared mailbox, creating unnecessary exposure to historical personal and financial communications.

risks.risk_name: Uncontrolled Data Access After Employee Departure
risks.description: There are no defined procedures for handling customer data stored on employee personal accounts when they leave their role or the company. Data could remain accessible on personal OneDrive accounts indefinitely.

risks.risk_name: Customer PII in Personal Cloud Storage
risks.description: Quality audit scorecards containing customer names and account numbers are stored in personal OneDrive accounts, with an estimated 8,000-10,000 customer records accumulated over time outside corporate data governance.

risks.risk_name: Unencrypted Email Transmission of Sensitive Customer Data
risks.description: Customer personal data including names, loan account numbers, mobile numbers, and potentially sensitive fraud-related responses are being transmitted via unencrypted email attachments. Monthly consolidated Excel reports containing this data are sent to Field Audit teams without encryption or password protection.

risks.risk_name: Unrestricted Data Export Capability
risks.description: Team leads and managers can export entire customer database including PAN, Aadhaar, and financial data to CSV files with no data loss prevention controls.

risks.risk_name: Inadequate Identity Verification for Financial Data Disclosure
risks.description: Customer financial data including loan account numbers, EMI amounts, payment history, and outstanding balances are disclosed to callers based solely on caller ID matching without additional identity verification. This creates risk of unauthorized access if someone else uses the customer's registered phone.

risks.risk_name: Excessive Data Access Without Row-Level Security
risks.description: All 45 personnel can access any customer's complete financial profile including PAN, addresses, loan details, payment history, and case history without restrictions. This violates data minimization principles.

risks.risk_name: Misleading Call Recording Consent Notice
risks.description: IVR states calls 'may be recorded' but all calls are actually recorded without exception and without opt-out option. This constitutes misleading consent under DPDPA requirements.

risks.risk_name: Misleading Call Recording Consent
risks.description: IVR disclaimer states calls 'may be recorded' but all calls are actually recorded without exception, and no opt-out mechanism is provided to customers.

risks.risk_name: Lack of Data Privacy Consent for Outbound Calls
risks.description: Customers are not informed that their compliance call responses will be recorded and shared with other teams. There is no formal data privacy notice provided during outbound compliance calls, and customers are unaware of how their data will be used or shared.

risks.risk_name: Inconsistent Call Recording Disclosure
risks.description: Outbound compliance calls are recorded without consistent disclosure to customers. While agents are supposed to verbally mention call recording, this practice is not consistently followed, creating consent gaps for call recordings.

risks.risk_name: Customer Data in Quality Audit Records
risks.description: Quality audit scorecards contain customer identifiers (names, account numbers) linked to call recordings, creating additional processing of customer personal data without clear governance. This data is stored in Excel trackers for quality management purposes.

risks.risk_name: Lack of Call Recording Consent for Outbound Calls
risks.description: Outbound compliance calls are being recorded without proper customer consent or notification, as there is no automated disclaimer and verbal notification by agents is inconsistent.

risks.risk_name: Absence of Data Privacy Notice for Compliance Calls
risks.description: Customers are not informed about how their compliance call data will be collected, used, or shared with other teams during outbound verification calls.

risks.risk_name: Customer Identifiers in Quality Audit Data
risks.description: Quality audit scorecards contain customer identifiers (names, account numbers) linked to call recordings, creating additional processing of customer personal data without clear governance.

risks.risk_name: Unmasked PAN Display to All Staff
risks.description: Full PAN numbers of both primary applicants and co-applicants are displayed without masking to all Customer Care staff, creating unnecessary exposure of sensitive financial identifiers.

risks.risk_name: Potential Cross-Border Data Transfer via Ameyo
risks.description: Call recordings containing customer financial and personal data may be stored outside India through Ameyo's cloud infrastructure, with hosting location unverified despite vendor claims of India hosting.

risks.risk_name: Unmasked PAN Display
risks.description: Full PAN numbers are displayed unmasked to all Customer Care staff, creating unnecessary exposure of sensitive financial identifiers.

risks.risk_name: No Field-Level Access Controls
risks.description: All Customer Care profiles have identical read access to sensitive customer data including PAN, Aadhaar, and financial information, violating principle of least privilege.

risks.risk_name: Potential Cross-Border Transfer via Ameyo
risks.description: Call recordings containing financial and personal data may be stored outside India through Ameyo's cloud infrastructure, with hosting location unverified.

risks.risk_name: Excessive Access to Sensitive Complaint Data
risks.description: All customer care agents have unrestricted access to highly sensitive bribery complaint cases containing whistleblower information, employee allegations, and supporting evidence. This violates principle of least privilege and creates risk of data misuse or retaliation.

risks.risk_name: Unrestricted PAN Exposure to All Agents
risks.description: Full PAN numbers are visible to all 45 customer service personnel without masking or role-based restrictions. Customer care agents do not require PAN access for routine queries, creating unnecessary exposure of sensitive financial identifiers.

risks.risk_name: Unrestricted PAN Access
risks.description: Full PAN numbers are visible to all agents without masking or role-based restrictions. Approximately 45 people including agents, team leads, compliance, and quality teams can access any customer's complete PAN number.

risks.risk_name: Indefinite Retention of Sensitive Call Recordings
risks.description: Call recordings containing highly sensitive personal and financial data are retained indefinitely without defined retention policy. Recordings include discussions of financial hardship, medical emergencies, and personal circumstances.

risks.risk_name: Indefinite Call Recording Retention
risks.description: Call recordings containing sensitive personal and financial data are retained indefinitely without a defined retention policy, with recordings accessible from over two years ago.

risks.risk_name: Excessive Data Retention on Personal Devices
risks.description: Customer data including names, account numbers, mobile numbers, and sensitive compliance responses are stored indefinitely on personal OneDrive accounts. Data dating back to April 2024 (over a year) is retained without defined retention policies or secure deletion procedures.

risks.risk_name: Indefinite Call Recording Retention
risks.description: Call recordings containing customer financial and personal data are stored indefinitely with no retention policy or auto-deletion configured, with recordings accessible from March 2024 (nearly 2 years).

risks.risk_name: No Call Recording Retention Policy
risks.description: Call recordings containing customer financial and personal data are stored indefinitely without any retention policy or auto-deletion mechanism.

risks.risk_name: Customer PII in Quality Audit Files
risks.description: Quality audit scorecards stored in OneDrive contain customer names and account numbers, creating an additional repository of customer identifiers without clear retention controls.

risks.risk_name: No Data Retention Policy for Call Recordings
risks.description: Call recordings are stored indefinitely without any retention policy or auto-deletion, with recordings accessible from March 2024 (nearly 2 years) containing sensitive customer conversations.

risks.risk_name: Excessive Data Access Scope
risks.description: 45 personnel have unrestricted access to all customer records including PAN, addresses, financial details, and case history without row-level restrictions.

risks.risk_name: Indefinite Data Retention Without Policy
risks.description: Compliance calling data including customer personal information is being retained indefinitely on OneDrive without a defined retention policy or data disposal process.

evidence: Ameyo is integrated with Salesforce. When a call comes in, the system checks the caller's number against our customer database
Ameyo passes the caller's phone number to Salesforce via a CTI connector, and Salesforce does a lookup against the customer database
CTI connector passes caller phone number to Salesforce for auto-lookup
The agent gets a Salesforce screen with the customer's profile
Smita's team pulls recordings from Ameyo, listens to them, and scores the agents on a quality scorecard
